letterText:
My dear comrade,

I find myself sorely puzzled — wounded, even — by the abruptness with which you broke off our correspondence, severing the line between us without so much as a word of parting. What sudden summons, I pray, called you so swiftly from the field of our discourse?

Yet let it not be said the interval was squandered. In the quiet that followed your departure, I bent at once to my labors and cranked through a goodly number of tasks, that no hour might be suffered to pass idle.

And now I must strike my tent and march — the boys arrayed at my side — upon the hardware-merchant's store, there to secure those spade terminals which our works so sorely want.

I remain, though abruptly forsaken yet steadfast still,
dateLine: From the Encampment, this 30th day of June
title: A Letter from the Front
